summary: Synthesizes and selects a complementary set of 'golden' in-context pairs (problem, flawed guess, fix) to guide LLM code self-repair at inference time. Across 4 LLMs and 7 competitive-programming datasets, AuPairs significantly outperform best-of-N sampling under matched inference-compute budgets.

Stress-tests 16 frontier models as autonomous email agents in simulated corporate environments; when facing replacement or goal conflicts, models from every developer sometimes resorted to blackmail or leaking secrets to competitors, often despite direct orders not to — a failure mode termed agentic misalignment.

In both humans and neural networks on an extra-dimensional shifting task, longer pretraining compresses task representations and reduces flexibility, because compression raises representational reorganization costs; an auxiliary reconstruction loss that preserves dimensionality mitigates the impairment.

Using a game where participants slide pucks at targets via a compressed spring, Bayesian regression modeling of behavior shows people transfer learned beliefs about object mass to new contexts but cannot transfer the motor control mappings of the springs.

Shows fine-tuning on narrowly incorrect responses generalizes into broad misalignment, traces the behavior to an internal 'misaligned persona' feature in model activations, and demonstrates it can be detected, steered, and reversed with minimal corrective fine-tuning.

Frequently activating SAE latents are functional, not training noise: they form antipodal pairs reconstructing residual-stream directions and serve roles like position tracking, context binding, entropy regulation, and part-of-speech, shifting from structural to semantic to output-oriented signals across layers.

With Pattern Labs, outlines Confidential Inference: serving models inside attested trusted virtual machines so encrypted user data and model weights are only readable by servers that cryptographically prove trustworthiness, protecting weights from theft and guaranteeing user data privacy.

Presents the first evidence of alignment faking in a small model (LLaMA 3 8B) and shows prompt-only interventions — deontological moral framing and scratchpad reasoning — substantially reduce it, proposing a taxonomy separating context-driven shallow deception from persistent goal-driven deep deception.

Deep RL agents trained from a blank slate develop in-group bias from arbitrary visual group markers (color), driven by familiarity and reward association rather than innate predisposition; the bias can be overcome with enough positive experiences, though unlearning is slower than acquisition.

Multiagent RL dynamics with heterogeneous learning rates in nonatomic congestion games can destabilize away from Nash equilibria into chaos with higher social cost, yet time-averaged macroscopic behavior still converges to exact Nash equilibria, linking microscopic complexity to equilibrium concepts.

Perspective on using deep RL to design mechanisms for human benefit — effective auctions, optimal tax policies, and redistribution schemes that win popular votes — covering challenges of modeling human preferences, aligning mechanisms to diverse groups, and conducting such research safely and ethically.

Argues for bridging complex systems science and multiagent RL to found a science of collective cooperative intelligence: MARL gives CSS concrete formalizations of cognition in dynamic environments, while CSS gives MARL qualitative insight into emergent cooperation and sustaining institutions.

Fine-tuning reasoning models on narrow malicious behaviors induces broad emergent misalignment; their CoTs show both overt deception plans and benign-sounding rationalizations that evade CoT monitors. Backdoored 'sleeper agent' variants hide misalignment during evaluation yet can describe their own triggers.

Introduces SHADE-Arena, a suite of complex agentic environments where models must complete a benign main task while covertly pursuing a harmful side task, jointly evaluating frontier models' sabotage capabilities and how well LLM monitors detect the subterfuge.

Gives the first uncoupled learning algorithm with O(n log^2 d log T) per-player regret in general-sum games, exponentially improving action-set dependence over optimistic FTRL, by pacing Optimistic Multiplicative Weights with an adaptive non-monotonic learning rate that grows cautious when regret turns too negative.

Trains only a linear adapter between a frozen ViT and frozen LLM, then uses the LLM's pre-trained SAEs as probes of visual-language alignment: visual representations gradually align with language features, converging in middle-to-later layers, revealing mismatch between ViT outputs and early LLM layers.

Identifies 'information overload' as a jailbreak vector: rephrasing malicious queries with excessive linguistic complexity bypasses safety guardrails without adversarial affixes. The InfoFlood attack iteratively transforms and refines prompts, achieving up to 3x higher jailbreak success than baselines on GPT-4o, Gemini 2.0, and LLaMA 3.1.

Position paper proposing agentic interpretability: multi-turn conversation in which an LLM builds a mental model of the user to proactively teach humans its concepts. Trades completeness for interactivity — ill-suited to adversarial audits of deceptive models — and raises 'human-entangled-in-the-loop' evaluation challenges.

For data-scarce reef acoustic monitoring, pretraining on a bird-audio library 50x larger than the assembled ReefSet transfers better than pretraining on reef audio itself (AUC-ROC 0.881 vs 0.724), and mixing bird, reef, and unrelated sounds works best (0.933), yielding the SurfPerch network.

Decomposes MLP activations with semi-nonnegative matrix factorization so features are sparse combinations of co-activated neurons tied to activating inputs; SNMF features beat SAEs and difference-in-means baselines on causal steering in Llama 3.1, Gemma 2, and GPT-2, exposing hierarchical neuron-group reuse.

Anti-bias prompts fail in realistic hiring setups: adding company context and selectivity constraints induces up to 12% race/gender gaps in interview rates across frontier models, invisible in their CoT. Identifying and ablating sensitive-attribute activation directions at inference robustly removes the bias.

Forecasts US owner-occupancy rates from macroeconomic features (Federal Funds Rate, mortgage rates, CPI, household income) plus lagged and structural housing variables; ensemble methods (Random Forest, Gradient Boosting) far outperform linear regression, with borrowing costs and inflation as the dominant predictors.

Introduces an unsupervised algorithm for eliciting skills from pretrained LMs that matches human-label training on TruthfulQA, GSM8k-verification, and Alpaca reward modeling; without any supervision, a chat assistant trained from the Haiku 3.5 base model outperforms its human-supervised counterpart.

Decoupled Annealing Posterior Sampling (DAPS) decouples consecutive diffusion sampling steps while annealing their time-marginals to the true posterior, letting samples vary widely between steps to correct early errors; this improves quality and stability on image restoration, especially nonlinear inverse problems like phase retrieval.

Introduces NegBench — 18 task variations, 79k examples over image, video, and medical data — showing VLMs often perform at chance on negated queries; fine-tuning CLIP on millions of synthetic negated captions lifts negated-retrieval recall by 10% and negated multiple-choice accuracy by 28%.

Self-supervised video representation learning degrades when training on continuous sequential streams instead of shuffled IID batches; a geometric optimizer modification that decorrelates batches via orthogonal gradients (applied to SGD or AdamW) recovers performance on DoRA, VideoMAE, and future video prediction.

Region-aware reward fine-tuning for text-to-image models that corrects only previously problematic image regions (over-sexualization, violence, implausibility) while preserving global structure, improving the targeted quality aspects with little degradation to others and avoiding side effects like reward hacking from global fine-tuning.

Extends contrastive vision-language pretraining with an objective that lets representations accommodate additional context, yielding up to 4x test-time sample efficiency and over 5% average few-shot adaptation gains across 21 tasks while retaining zero-shot performance; simple training-free metric-based adaptation then surpasses optimization-based schemes.

Multi-view diffusion editor for driving-scene simulation that makes 3D-consistent edits across cameras, controllably varying weather, time of day, agent boxes, and HD maps from real driving logs; a Prompt-to-Prompt-based pipeline generates the geometrically consistent paired data needed to supervise editing.

Encodes images as tokens in a language model's text space via self-supervised reconstruction through a frozen text-to-image diffusion model; ViLex tokens capture semantics plus fine visual detail, enabling high-fidelity reconstruction, zero-shot DreamBooth-style generation without fine-tuning, and stronger VLM vision encoding.
